lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਰਦ ਸਾਰਦ ਕਰਹਿ ਖਵਾਸੀ ॥
lines.translit: naarad saarad kareh khavaasee |
lines.english: Naarada the sage, and Shaarada the goddess of knowledge, serve the Lord.

lines.gurmukhi: ਪਾਸਿ ਬੈਠੀ ਬੀਬੀ ਕਵਲਾ ਦਾਸੀ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: paas baitthee beebee kavalaa daasee |2|
lines.english: The goddess Lakhshmi sits by Him as His slave. ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਹੰਸ ਨਾਮੁ ਲੈ ਲੈ ਕਰਉ ਸਲਾਮੁ ॥੩॥
lines.translit: sahans naam lai lai krau salaam |3|
lines.english: I repeat the Naam, the Name of the Lord, a thousand times, and bow in reverence to Him. ||3||

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਹਤ ਕਬੀਰ ਰਾਮ ਗੁਨ ਗਾਵਉ ॥
lines.translit: kehat kabeer raam gun gaavau |
lines.english: Says Kabeer, I sing the Glorious Praises of the Lord;

lines.gurmukhi: ਕੰਠੇ ਮਾਲਾ ਜਿਹਵਾ ਰਾਮੁ ॥
lines.translit: kantthe maalaa jihavaa raam |
lines.english: The mala is around my neck, and the Lord's Name is upon my tongue.

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਿੰਦੂ ਤੁਰਕ ਦੋਊ ਸਮਝਾਵਉ ॥੪॥੪॥੧੩॥
lines.translit: hindoo turak doaoo samajhaavau |4|4|13|
lines.english: I teach both Hindus and Muslims. ||4||4||13||

lines.gurmukhi: ਆਸਾ ਸ੍ਰੀ ਕਬੀਰ ਜੀਉ ਕੇ ਪੰਚਪਦੇ ੯ ਦੁਤੁਕੇ ੫ ॥
lines.translit: aasaa sree kabeer jeeo ke panchapade 9 dutuke 5 |
lines.english: Aasaa, Kabeer Jee, 9 Panch-Padhay, 5 Dho-Thukay:

lines.gurmukhi: ੴ ਸਤਿਗੁਰ ਪ੍ਰਸਾਦਿ ॥
lines.translit: ik oankaar satigur prasaad |
lines.english: One Universal Creator God. By The Grace Of The True Guru:

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਿਸੁ ਪਾਹਨ ਕਉ ਪਾਤੀ ਤੋਰੈ ਸੋ ਪਾਹਨ ਨਿਰਜੀਉ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: jis paahan kau paatee torai so paahan nirajeeo |1|
lines.english: That stone idol, for which you tear off those leaves - that stone idol is lifeless. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਪਾਤੀ ਤੋਰੈ ਮਾਲਿਨੀ ਪਾਤੀ ਪਾਤੀ ਜੀਉ ॥
lines.translit: paatee torai maalinee paatee paatee jeeo |
lines.english: You tear off the leaves, O gardener, but in each and every leaf, there is life.

lines.gurmukhi: ਭੂਲੀ ਮਾਲਨੀ ਹੈ ਏਉ ॥
lines.translit: bhoolee maalanee hai eo |
lines.english: In this, you are mistaken, O gardener.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਤਿਗੁਰੁ ਜਾਗਤਾ ਹੈ ਦੇਉ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: satigur jaagataa hai deo |1| rahaau |
lines.english: The True Guru is the Living Lord. ||1||Pause||

lines.gurmukhi: ਬ੍ਰਹਮੁ ਪਾਤੀ ਬਿਸਨੁ ਡਾਰੀ ਫੂਲ ਸੰਕਰਦੇਉ ॥
lines.translit: braham paatee bisan ddaaree fool sankaradeo |
lines.english: Brahma is in the leaves, Vishnu is in the branches, and Shiva is in the flowers.

lines.gurmukhi: ਤੀਨਿ ਦੇਵ ਪ੍ਰਤਖਿ ਤੋਰਹਿ ਕਰਹਿ ਕਿਸ ਕੀ ਸੇਉ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: teen dev pratakh toreh kareh kis kee seo |2|
lines.english: When you break these three gods, whose service are you performing? ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਪਾਖਾਨ ਗਢਿ ਕੈ ਮੂਰਤਿ ਕੀਨੑੀ ਦੇ ਕੈ ਛਾਤੀ ਪਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: paakhaan gadt kai moorat keenaee de kai chhaatee paau |
lines.english: The sculptor carves the stone and fashions it into an idol, placing his feet upon its chest.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜੇ ਏਹ ਮੂਰਤਿ ਸਾਚੀ ਹੈ ਤਉ ਗੜ੍ਹਣਹਾਰੇ ਖਾਉ ॥੩॥
lines.translit: je eeh moorat saachee hai tau garrhanahaare khaau |3|
lines.english: If this stone god was true, it would devour the sculptor for this! ||3||

lines.gurmukhi: ਭੋਗਨਹਾਰੇ ਭੋਗਿਆ ਇਸੁ ਮੂਰਤਿ ਕੇ ਮੁਖ ਛਾਰੁ ॥੪॥
lines.translit: bhoganahaare bhogiaa is moorat ke mukh chhaar |4|
lines.english: - the priest enjoys these, while he puts ashes into the mouth of the idol. ||4||

lines.gurmukhi: ਭਾਤੁ ਪਹਿਤਿ ਅਰੁ ਲਾਪਸੀ ਕਰਕਰਾ ਕਾਸਾਰੁ ॥
lines.translit: bhaat pahit ar laapasee karakaraa kaasaar |
lines.english: Rice and beans, candies, cakes and cookies

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਾਲਿਨਿ ਭੂਲੀ ਜਗੁ ਭੁਲਾਨਾ ਹਮ ਭੁਲਾਨੇ ਨਾਹਿ ॥
lines.translit: maalin bhoolee jag bhulaanaa ham bhulaane naeh |
lines.english: The gardener is mistaken, and the world is mistaken, but I am not mistaken.

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਹੁ ਕਬੀਰ ਹਮ ਰਾਮ ਰਾਖੇ ਕ੍ਰਿਪਾ ਕਰਿ ਹਰਿ ਰਾਇ ॥੫॥੧॥੧੪॥
lines.translit: kahu kabeer ham raam raakhe kripaa kar har raae |5|1|14|
lines.english: Says Kabeer, the Lord preserves me; the Lord, my King, has showered His Blessings upon me. ||5||1||14||

lines.gurmukhi: ਆਸਾ ॥
lines.translit: aasaa |
lines.english: Aasaa:

lines.gurmukhi: ਤੀਸ ਬਰਸ ਕਛੁ ਦੇਵ ਨ ਪੂਜਾ ਫਿਰਿ ਪਛੁਤਾਨਾ ਬਿਰਧਿ ਭਇਓ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: tees baras kachh dev na poojaa fir pachhutaanaa biradh bheo |1|
lines.english: For another thirty years, he does not worship God in any way, and then, when he is old, he repents and regrets. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਬਾਰਹ ਬਰਸ ਬਾਲਪਨ ਬੀਤੇ ਬੀਸ ਬਰਸ ਕਛੁ ਤਪੁ ਨ ਕੀਓ ॥
lines.translit: baarah baras baalapan beete bees baras kachh tap na keeo |
lines.english: Twelve years pass in childhood, and for another twenty years, he does not practice self-discipline and austerity.

lines.gurmukhi: ਮੇਰੀ ਮੇਰੀ ਕਰਤੇ ਜਨਮੁ ਗਇਓ ॥
lines.translit: meree meree karate janam geo |
lines.english: His life wastes away as he cries out, "Mine, mine!"

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਾਇਰੁ ਸੋਖਿ ਭੁਜੰ ਬਲਇਓ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: saaeir sokh bhujan baleo |1| rahaau |
lines.english: The pool of his power has dried up. ||1||Pause||

lines.gurmukhi: ਸੂਕੇ ਸਰਵਰਿ ਪਾਲਿ ਬੰਧਾਵੈ ਲੂਣੈ ਖੇਤਿ ਹਥ ਵਾਰਿ ਕਰੈ ॥
lines.translit: sooke saravar paal bandhaavai loonai khet hath vaar karai |
lines.english: He makes a dam around the dried-up pool, and with his hands, he makes a fence around the harvested field.

lines.gurmukhi: ਆਇਓ ਚੋਰੁ ਤੁਰੰਤਹ ਲੇ ਗਇਓ ਮੇਰੀ ਰਾਖਤ ਮੁਗਧੁ ਫਿਰੈ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: aaeo chor turantah le geo meree raakhat mugadh firai |2|
lines.english: When the thief of Death comes, he quickly carries away what the fool had tried to preserve as his own. ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਚਰਨ ਸੀਸੁ ਕਰ ਕੰਪਨ ਲਾਗੇ ਨੈਨੀ ਨੀਰੁ ਅਸਾਰ ਬਹੈ ॥
lines.translit: charan sees kar kanpan laage nainee neer asaar bahai |
lines.english: His feet and head and hands begin to tremble, and the tears flow copiously from his eyes.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਿਹਵਾ ਬਚਨੁ ਸੁਧੁ ਨਹੀ ਨਿਕਸੈ ਤਬ ਰੇ ਧਰਮ ਕੀ ਆਸ ਕਰੈ ॥੩॥
lines.translit: jihavaa bachan sudh nahee nikasai tab re dharam kee aas karai |3|
lines.english: His tongue has not spoken the correct words, but now, he hopes to practice religion! ||3||

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਰਿ ਜੀਉ ਕ੍ਰਿਪਾ ਕਰੈ ਲਿਵ ਲਾਵੈ ਲਾਹਾ ਹਰਿ ਹਰਿ ਨਾਮੁ ਲੀਓ ॥
lines.translit: har jeeo kripaa karai liv laavai laahaa har har naam leeo |
lines.english: If the Dear Lord shows His Mercy, one enshrines love for Him, and obtains the Profit of the Lord's Name.

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਰ ਪਰਸਾਦੀ ਹਰਿ ਧਨੁ ਪਾਇਓ ਅੰਤੇ ਚਲਦਿਆ ਨਾਲਿ ਚਲਿਓ ॥੪॥
lines.translit: gur parasaadee har dhan paaeo ante chaladiaa naal chalio |4|
lines.english: By Guru's Grace, he receives the wealth of the Lord's Name, which alone shall go with him, when he departs in the end. ||4||

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਹਤ ਕਬੀਰ ਸੁਨਹੁ ਰੇ ਸੰਤਹੁ ਅਨੁ ਧਨੁ ਕਛੂਐ ਲੈ ਨ ਗਇਓ ॥
lines.translit: kehat kabeer sunahu re santahu an dhan kachhooai lai na geo |
lines.english: Says Kabeer, listen, O Saints - he shall not take any other wealth with him.

lines.gurmukhi: ਆਈ ਤਲਬ ਗੋਪਾਲ ਰਾਇ ਕੀ ਮਾਇਆ ਮੰਦਰ ਛੋਡਿ ਚਲਿਓ ॥੫॥੨॥੧੫॥
lines.translit: aaee talab gopaal raae kee maaeaa mandar chhodd chalio |5|2|15|
lines.english: When the summons comes from the King, the Lord of the Universe, the mortal departs, leaving behind his wealth and mansions. ||5||2||15||

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਾਹੂ ਗਰੀ ਗੋਦਰੀ ਨਾਹੀ ਕਾਹੂ ਖਾਨ ਪਰਾਰਾ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: kaahoo garee godaree naahee kaahoo khaan paraaraa |1|
lines.english: Some do not even have a poor patched coat, and some live in thatched huts. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਆਸਾ ॥
lines.translit: aasaa |
lines.english: Aasaa:

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਾਹੂ ਦੀਨੑੇ ਪਾਟ ਪਟੰਬਰ ਕਾਹੂ ਪਲਘ ਨਿਵਾਰਾ ॥
lines.translit: kaahoo deenae paatt pattanbar kaahoo palagh nivaaraa |
lines.english: To some, the Lord has given silks and satins, and to some, beds decorated with cotton ribbons.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸੁਕ੍ਰਿਤੁ ਕਰਿ ਕਰਿ ਲੀਜੈ ਰੇ ਮਨ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: sukrit kar kar leejai re man |1| rahaau |
lines.english: By continually doing good deeds, these are obtained, O my mind. ||1||Pause||

lines.gurmukhi: ਕੁਮੑਾਰੈ ਏਕ ਜੁ ਮਾਟੀ ਗੂੰਧੀ ਬਹੁ ਬਿਧਿ ਬਾਨੀ ਲਾਈ ॥
lines.translit: kumaarai ek ju maattee goondhee bahu bidh baanee laaee |
lines.english: The potter works the same clay, and colors the pots in different ways.

lines.gurmukhi: ਅਹਿਰਖ ਵਾਦੁ ਨ ਕੀਜੈ ਰੇ ਮਨ ॥
lines.translit: ahirakh vaad na keejai re man |
lines.english: Do not indulge in envy and bickering, O my mind.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸੂਮਹਿ ਧਨੁ ਰਾਖਨ ਕਉ ਦੀਆ ਮੁਗਧੁ ਕਹੈ ਧਨੁ ਮੇਰਾ ॥
lines.translit: soomeh dhan raakhan kau deea mugadh kahai dhan meraa |
lines.english: God gave wealth to the miser for him to preserve, but the fool calls it his own.

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਾਹੂ ਮਹਿ ਮੋਤੀ ਮੁਕਤਾਹਲ ਕਾਹੂ ਬਿਆਧਿ ਲਗਾਈ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: kaahoo meh motee mukataahal kaahoo biaadh lagaaee |2|
lines.english: Into some, he sets pearls, while to others, he attaches filth. ||2||
